target.name: Wiliot
target.founder: Tal Tamir, Yaron Elboim, Alon Yehezkely

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $230M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Wiliot is a Series C semiconductor company that has raised $230M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public comparables in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, Positron AI, and DEEPX as primary private reference points.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $230M total raised, implying a $391M valuation. Peer-median anchoring relies on a 4-company same-sector cohort spanning Series B through Series D, which yields a $1.9B median valuation with a range of $529M to $5.0B. Public-comparable anchoring cannot be applied because Wiliot's revenue is not disclosed, precluding use of the 25.3x median price-to-sales multiple observed across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD. 

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $332M to $2.1B, with a median of $1.1B. The wide dispersion reflects stage heterogeneity within the peer set and uncertainty around Wiliot's revenue scale and operating performance relative to better-capitalized competitors. Stage-progression implies the lower bound, while peer-median anchoring dominates the upper bound. Key uncertainties include Wiliot's path to revenue traction, gross margins in ultra-low-power IoT semiconductors, and competitive positioning against larger-capitalized peers.
